rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD COLE WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) The report (no courtesy copy was provided) does not fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. and does not disclose why the estate has not yet closed, as required by law. Deadline is 3/29/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAMUEL V. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/23/2024. No response to notes sent 12/08/2023 and 01/25/2024. Issues remaining include: 1) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance (San Francisco Local Rule 14.34.D). 2) The account as presented does not balance. Total charges are $872,336.98 while total credits are $865,875.45, a difference of $6,461.53. 3) Please describe in more detail the deposits for various amounts on Schedule A. Receipts, showing the nature of purpose of each item, the source of the receipt, and the date of receipt must be described in Schedule A (Probate Code Section 1064(a)). 4) No relief is requested and no information whatsoever is included in the petition. Please review the requirements set forth under Probate Code Section 1064 (and CRC 7.651 and 7.652 if this petition is intended to be a petition for final distribution). 5) No proposed order has been received (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Issues to be addressed and all filings to be filed no later than 04/09/2024.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee, For Accounting, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And Surcharge
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The parties to report the court with staus of the case. The following issues have not been cleared: 1. Petition prays transfer of assets to Trust B and damages under sec. 859. Appears the petition includes the claim under sec. 850. Notice of Hearing On Petition To Determine Claim to Property must be served pursuant to sec. 851, especially on Yafim Ratinski (the trustee of Trust A) and Minna Ferna (the temp trustee of Trust B). 2. Notice must be served on the successor-in-interest of Mikhail Vaynberg who is/was the beneficiary. Notice on file showed the service was made on his attorney in this proceeding. Petition may need to address whether there is any personal representative was appointed in Israel or in the US, whichever applicable. 3. Caption needs to reflect all relief being sought. CRC 7.102. Then, the notice needs to sufficiently describe the caption. CRC 7.50. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENIO S. AMADOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (updated and e-mailed to counsel on 01/10/2024) since the initial hearing on 10/03/2023: 1) All heirs of the decedent's predeceased spouse who would take 50% of the estate real property under Probate Code section 6402.5(a)(1)-(3) must be identified and receive notice, as the predeceased spouse's interest in the real property passed to the decedent as the surviving joint tenant. See also section 6402.5(f)(3) & (4). A separate petition under section 11700 is generally required before final distribution to determine additional heirs who were not disclosed at the outset of the proceedings. LRSF 14.35(H). If the predeceased spouse was survived by her parents, siblings, or their issue (niece, nephew, etc.), they must be identified. If the predeceased spouse was not survived by her parents, siblings, or their issue, a family tree showing the names and dates of death of all deceased parents, siblings, and their issue must be provided. 2) A financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed. LRSF 14.34(D). The American Business Bank account statement shows the balance of $660,364.29 as of 07/05/2023, but Schedule 6 shows $660,371.53. The difference must be explained. A declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. 3) Please provide supporting time records for the attorney's ordinary services. If no time records were kept, please provide categories of ordinary services provided and hours spent on each category. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK HOSKING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The proof of service filed on 2/13/24, however, shows that notice of the second amendment was mailed on 2/13/24, BEFORE that pleading was e-filed on 2/15/24. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The Court waives defective notice as the net amount of cash distributable to the heirs has not changed. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The footer must contain the title of the paper (examples: "Complaint," "XYZ Corp.'s Motion for Summary Judgment") or some clear and concise abbreviation. [CRC 2.110(b).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER MORRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/16/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) Please file the receipt from Peninsula Guns re the sale of the firearms. (3) The full amount of the $1,273.21 claim of AT&T Mobility was allowed, but only $763.93 was paid? No satisfaction of the claim appears to have been filed. (4) The full amount of Laura Krause's claim was rejected, but she was paid $20,297.88? (5) The Probate Referee's commission on the I&A was $916.35, but she was paid a total of $926.26? (6) Attorney costs paid on 11/8/23 must be itemized. (7) The administrator made a loan of $500 but was reimbursed $94,683.34. The property tax, mortgage, insurance, and bond payments from 12/24/22 - 8/28/23 must be set forth in detail. (8) For what additional legal services (provided to whom?) did Nathan James bill and was paid $1,649.01 through 5/31/23? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZHAOHONG YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/02/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The omnibus clause has not been corrected to identify the decedent's mother as the sole distributee. 2) If the corrected inventory and appraisal added receipts (such as interest accrued or any refund issued after the decedent's death), it must be corrected to show the decedent's assets as they existed as of the date of death. If the inventory and appraisal filed on 05/19/2023 (and presumably superseded by the corrected inventory and appraisal subsequently filed) correctly listed the date-of-death value, that value is the fee base used to calculate the attorney's statutory compensation. To the extent the fee base alleged in the petition included receipts, a detailed schedule of receipts required by CRC 7.550(b)(6) must be filed. 3) Is the petitioner the only sibling the decedent ever had? The information is required to determine under Probate Code section 282 whether the petitioner is the only taker of the interest in the assets on hand disclaimed by the decedent's mother (who was the sole heir).

Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether the proposed budget satisfies Local Rule 14.49B. 2) Petitioner requests authority for trustee to invest in mutual trusts. The proposed trustee's investment strategy is provided, but the mutual funds are not specified, as required under Local Rule 14.49C. 3) The Trustee resides in Pennsylvania and the trust beneficiary resides in Santa Rosa. Court to determine where the proper venue should be going forward. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Invalidate Teh Williams Family Trust Of Betty Yvonne Williams And Leroy Williams And To Remove The Appointed Trustee, Shawny K.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: 1) No notice of hearing is on file. At least 30 days before the time set for hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 to all trustees and all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203). Form DE-120 is required for this proceeding. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) No proposed order has been received (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A) 5) No amended petition curing the defects sustained on the demurrer has been filed. See Order After Hearing issued on December 18, 2023. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JANNE LIU YIU CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/02/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/18/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) An authenticated copy of the Hong Kong Court's order (grant) admitting the will to probate must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached. An original apostille must be attached. See LRSF 14.22; see also Probate Codes section 12521(a)(2) & (b). 2) Notice must be given to the Chinese Consulate, as required by section 8113. 3) Item 1 of the DE-121 form must specify all names by which the decedent was known, including Janne Liu Yiu-Chu and Dorothy Yiu-Chu Liu. New notice must be mailed and published.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JORGE EFRAIN MONROY REVOCABLE living TRUST DATED APRIl 3, 2017, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Title To Property (Probate Code Section 17200.1. 850 Set Seq)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended petition was not e-filed until 2/23/24, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. [Probate Code Section 851(a). (2) A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any HEIR-AT-LAW is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Probate Code Section 17203(b) and CCP Section 413.10 et seq.] Monica Monroy must be personally served notice of the 4/30/24 hearing of the amended petition or must waive notice. Deadline is 4/19/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE OZOLS LIVING TRUST DATED AUGUST 3, 2019
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Certain Real Property Is Held In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issue to be cleared/addressed: 1. Need an authority under which the court can confirm the petitioner's current ownership of the real property vested by the 2021 Grant Deed. The court's determination may be limited to whether the property belonged to the trust upon the 2019 Deed under Prob. Code sec. 850. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONG YEE CHIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) The Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities must be filed. (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) Item 8 must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must also be clearly identified and must receive notice. (5) Did the testator leave written directions disposing of items tangible personal property? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The Declaration of Real Property is no longer required by this Court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUANGNAM PONGGLUD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be supplemented before the hearing: 1. Need a verified supplemental declaration setting forth the date of death of the named beneficiaries (John Sullivan and Chuchep Pongglud) to confirm if each of them survived the decedent or not. 2. The court requires a minimum bond of $20,000 for a non-California resident fiduciary even though the decedent's will waives bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
